The substantive update remains in the existing source-specific connectors/ responsibility root; the paired receipt uses data/receipts/generated/. No root, lifecycle phase, schema home, policy home, registry home, or release authority is created.

The product contract was reconciled with connector-root and Kansas-family guidance, remaining aliases, source profile, three registry placeholders, SourceDescriptor schema, Soil moisture contract, and Mesonet normalizer documentation without claiming implementation.

Current official Kansas Mesonet REST, Data Usage, Network Architecture, Weather Parameters, and Soil Moisture pages were inspected on 2026-07-12; unstable access, interval, record-limit, variable-order, quality-flag, depth, citation, preliminary-data, and consent claims were updated.

The remote content identity is Git blob SHA-1 346fd5a5318ca6800d1d6bd9a846bec0ac35a511. artifact_hashes records SHA-256 2ba3bc5837e7b926e0c1bde4c43f49477f1b4e64c737f66e4f1f6df78536a299 over canonical identifier git-blob-sha1:346fd5a5318ca6800d1d6bd9a846bec0ac35a511; the Git object remains the byte-addressed source of truth.

notes: AI-authored documentation update on a scoped review branch. The user-named top-level alias was already deleted, so the branch updates the surviving Kansas-family product lane without recreating obsolete structure. The contract records official REST limits and parsing caveats, scopes written consent to automated ingest, preserves preliminary/revision and QA/QC state, and keeps path, registry, station-health, package, test, activation, and runtime uncertainty visible.
